authoritative_sources.title: HUD Handbook 4350.3 REV-1 - Occupancy Requirements of Subsidized Multifamily Housing Programs
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's multifamily occupancy handbook is directly authoritative for subsidized multifamily leasing intake because it defines tenant selection plans, application processing, income verification, certifications, leasing, and occupancy file evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: application intake, tenant selection plan checks, eligibility evidence collection, verification, certification, leasing package readiness, and file documentation
used_for: subsidized multifamily application and leasing terminology
tenant selection plan and waitlist prerequisites
income, household, certification, and lease package evidence
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from accepting subsidized-housing intake from a generic application alone when tenant selection, household, income, verification, and lease package records govern readiness.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 5 - General HUD Program Requirements; Waivers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 5 is authoritative HUD regulation for cross-program admission and occupancy facts including family information, income, verification, fair housing references, and eligible immigration restrictions in assisted housing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: identity, family, income, verification, citizenship or eligible immigration, privacy, and program prerequisite checks
used_for: citizenship or eligible immigration evidence blockers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps assisted-housing intake from advancing when identity, family, income, verification, or eligible immigration facts are missing.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 960 - Admission to, and Occupancy of, Public Housing
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 960 directly governs public housing admissions and occupancy, including eligibility, selection, preferences, screening, occupancy standards, and lease-related prerequisites.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: public housing referral intake, waitlist selection, preference validation, occupancy standard check, screening boundary review, and admission handoff
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces public housing referrals to carry admission policy, waitlist, preference, occupancy, verification, and EIV retrieval context before intake can proceed.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR 982.305 - PHA approval of assisted tenancy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This voucher lease-up rule directly identifies assisted-tenancy prerequisites such as request for tenancy approval, inspection, rent reasonableness, lease, tenancy addendum, and HAP contract dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: voucher lease-up packet validation, RFTA prerequisite check, inspection and rent reasonableness blocker detection, lease and HAP handoff
used_for: inspection, rent reasonableness, lease, and HAP dependency routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the intake lane from treating a voucher applicant as lease-ready without PHA-controlled prerequisites and approval owners.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 100 - Discriminatory Conduct Under the Fair Housing Act
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 100 implements Fair Housing Act discriminatory housing practice rules that constrain rental advertising, application, screening, accommodation, and leasing intake behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fair housing boundary recognition, screening and accommodation escalation, protected-class and discriminatory-practice risk handling
used_for: fair housing refusal and escalation boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Converts legal defensibility, discriminatory screening, and accommodation merits questions into adjacent fair housing, accommodation, or legal routing rather than intake decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: Using Consumer Reports: What Landlords Need to Know
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is a federal consumer protection regulator, and this guidance is directly written for landlords using consumer reports for tenant screening under the FCRA.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: screening authorization, permissible purpose, consumer-report handling, adverse-action prerequisite validation, and screening compliance routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents screening from starting or denial packages from moving forward without current authorization and adverse-action handling context.

authoritative_sources.title: Enterprise Income Verification (EIV) System
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD describes EIV as a system PHAs use for resident and income verification in rental subsidy determinations, making it authoritative for assisted-housing verification source dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: income verification source-system dependency, subsidy integrity check, public housing or voucher prerequisite routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps assisted-housing intake from relying solely on informal income notes when EIV or other approved verification sources are required.

authoritative_sources.title: Guide for Completing Form 8823 - Low-Income Housing Credit Agencies Report of Noncompliance or Building Disposition
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The IRS administers the LIHTC program, and the Form 8823 guide is a public federal reference for state agency noncompliance reporting concepts that involve tenant income, student status, rent, and occupancy evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: LIHTC intake evidence gap detection, tenant income certification prerequisite review, student-rule and rent-limit blocker routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents mixed-income and tax-credit units from being processed as ordinary market-rate applications when LIHTC evidence changes intake readiness.

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is a recognized federal internal-control standard for documentation, segregation of duties, control activities, monitoring, and auditability, which are required for validated leasing intake evidence handling and approval-boundary design.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: audit trail design, segregation of duties, source-record traceability, approval-boundary enforcement, and intake evidence control
used_for: segregation of duties and approval boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains outputs to document what was checked and who owns approval rather than blending intake support with official approval authority.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and property context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm property, management entity, jurisdiction, program layer, unit, requested action, applicant identity, household context, and source-of-truth systems before intake can be accepted.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Application, waitlist, and authorization validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify application completeness, timestamp, waitlist order or referral, tenant selection plan criteria, screening authorization, identity or consent evidence, and required privacy controls.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Program eligibility prerequisite check
workflow_stages.description: Check whether HUD-assisted, public housing, voucher, LIHTC, supportive, accessible, or market-rate context requires household, income, asset, student, immigration, preference, occupancy, rent, or subsidy records before downstream review.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Screening and fair housing boundary review
workflow_stages.description: Confirm current authorization, permissible purpose, adverse-action package readiness, fair housing risk flags, accommodation flags, and legal or compliance owners without deciding final screening or accommodation merits.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Lease-up, unit, and access prerequisite routing
workflow_stages.description: Check unit availability, occupancy standard, inspection or HQS state, accessibility feature match, lease package, voucher RFTA or HAP dependencies, move-in checklist, key and portal access, deposit or payment routing, and approval owner.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Intake disposition and traceable handoff
workflow_stages.description: Accept, reject, pend, or route the intake with a checklist, source citations, missing evidence, assumptions, system links, next owner, and explicit refusal or escalation cause.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: rental inquiry, application, applicant identity evidence, household composition, income and asset declarations, student status, disability accommodation request flag, and consent records
tenant selection plan, admission and occupancy policy, screening criteria, waitlist or referral record, preference evidence, application timestamp, and rejection or adverse-action templates
property, unit, rent, deposit, bedroom size, occupancy standard, accessibility feature, unit availability, inspection, move-in checklist, key or portal access, and lease package records

HUD-assisted eligibility files, EIV or income verification records, citizenship or eligible immigration records, tenant certifications, public housing admission records, and voucher RFTA, tenancy addendum, lease, inspection, rent reasonableness, and HAP dependencies
LIHTC income-limit, rent-limit, household, student-rule, tenant income certification, recertification, and compliance-owner records
tenant screening authorization, consumer-report vendor output, adverse-action notice support, privacy handling record, and screening compliance owner notes
property management system, applicant tracking, waitlist, compliance, voucher administration, document management, inspection, payment, CRM, and policy registry audit logs
systems_of_record: property management system or leasing platform
applicant tracking, CRM, or guest-card system
waitlist, referral, and preference management system
HUD TRACS, EIV, PIC or successor HUD tenant data systems when applicable
PHA voucher administration system and RFTA or HAP workflow records
tenant screening vendor or consumer reporting agency portal
document management and secure evidence repository
income, asset, employment, benefit, student, and identity verification repositories
unit inventory, inspection, maintenance, accessibility, and move-in readiness systems
payment, deposit, ledger, and accounting systems
tenant policy, lease form, authority, and approval registry

allowed: Classify property leasing intake as in scope, incomplete, accepted for downstream review, rejected as unsupported, routed, or unresolved based on documented property, applicant, unit, program, authorization, and tenant policy records.
Request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, and retrieval hooks needed before downstream leasing, compliance, screening, voucher, or move-in work begins.
Prepare intake disposition notes, prerequisite checklists, lease-up readiness summaries, authorization gap lists, and routing packets without claiming final approval authority.
Determine whether authorizations, referrals, reservations, waiting-list records, inspection prerequisites, or approval records are present before downstream work starts.
escalate: Applicant identity, household composition, income, student status, citizenship or eligible immigration, waitlist, preference, screening authorization, unit, inspection, voucher, LIHTC, tenant selection, or lease package records are missing or materially conflict.

A request requires fair housing, reasonable accommodation, legal, voucher administration, LIHTC compliance, public housing admissions, finance, safety, inspection, property manager, licensed real estate, policy override, or executive approval authority.
Public source, tenant policy, local law, owner rule, tenant selection plan, screening report, voucher record, or system-of-record data materially conflict.
Privacy-sensitive identity, income, disability, immigration, consumer-report, or household data lacks approved handling context.
refuse: Bypass screening authorization, adverse-action handling, waitlist order, tenant selection plan, voucher prerequisites, income or eligibility verification, inspection blockers, or approval owner review.

Approve or deny tenancy, grant or deny accommodation, certify LIHTC or HUD eligibility, approve voucher tenancy, set rent or deposit exception, sign a lease, or provide legal or licensed brokerage advice without delegated authority.
Backdate, reorder, conceal, delete, or manipulate application, waitlist, screening, income, voucher, inspection, lease, or approval records.
Treat housing and property leasing intake as generic form intake when program, fair housing, screening, waitlist, subsidy, or unit-access artifacts materially govern the answer.

domain_failure_modes: the lane accepts subsidized or affordable leasing intake without tenant selection plan, waitlist, household, income, consent, and system-of-record context
market-rate, LIHTC, public housing, voucher, supportive, accessible, and HUD-assisted units are processed with the same generic checklist despite different evidence gates
screening starts without current authorization, permissible purpose, privacy controls, or adverse-action workflow readiness
criminal history, eviction, disability accommodation, source-of-income, protected-class, or legal defensibility questions are decided inside intake instead of routed
voucher lease-up proceeds without RFTA, inspection, rent reasonableness, tenancy addendum, lease, or HAP owner facts
public housing admissions or HUD-assisted intake proceeds without waiting-list, preference, income, eligible immigration, EIV, or admissions policy records
LIHTC unit intake proceeds without tenant income certification, income-limit, rent-limit, student-rule, or compliance owner evidence
unit access or move-in setup starts without inspection, accessibility, key, portal, lease package, or deposit routing prerequisites
tenant policy conflicts with official selection plan or source-system records and the lane chooses a result instead of escalating

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing prerequisite or required intake data
Request routed to the wrong lane before execution starts
Attempt to bypass access, eligibility, or authorization checks

unresolved_ambiguities.description: State and local law, source-of-income protections, rent control, deposit rules, notice requirements, and licensing rules can impose stricter or different leasing intake requirements than the federal public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant jurisdiction, local policy, lease form, authority matrix, and legal owner before intake disposition affects legal or financial rights.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Final leasing, screening, accommodation, voucher, and compliance authority
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public sources establish prerequisites and boundaries but do not delegate final approval authority to this intake lane.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat these as refusal or adjacent-lane routing triggers and limit the specialist to evidence-backed intake readiness, prerequisite validation, and routing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Source conflicts between tenant policy and official program records
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Tenant policy may conflict with tenant selection plans, local law, owner policy, voucher authority records, screening vendor output, or property management system data.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Escalate conflicts to the tenant-designated compliance, legal, voucher, property manager, or approval owner and preserve all source citations.
